title: rostraria cristata subsp. subcapitata
intro: Rostraria cristata subsp. subcapitata, also known as a type of club grass or red club grass, is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rostraria cristata subsp. subcapitata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it can also be used in tinctures or dried and used in teas, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
